SOXX.data_anomaly: price_source_conflict: fast_info prev_close $639.99 vs history $599.73 diverge by 6.7%. Signal suppressed.

macro_summary: Monday's session saw the S&P 500 slip 0.03% while the Nasdaq eked out a 0.11% gain, as optimism from last week's Iran peace deal was overshadowed by renewed Hormuz closure and fresh China-U.S. trade retaliation. Oil remained volatile, with WTI at $75.32 after a further 0.49% dip; the Strait of Hormuz was closed again by Iran citing Lebanon ceasefire violations (per World Oil, June 20). China imposed export controls on 10 U.S. defense firms and procurement bans on 46 others (per CNBC, June 22), escalating the tech-tariff front ahead of Thursday's PCE inflation print. Futures pointed lower Sunday night (per CNBC), reflecting caution around the Fed's hawkish hold and a 75% market-implied chance of a rate hike as early as September (per Yahoo Finance). The latest CPI, however, remains stale at 4.3% YoY from the May release.
commodities_note: WTI held near $75 after the Hormuz closure, with analysts warning that disruptions could quickly reverse the recent oil plunge (per World Oil). Gold edged up 0.84% to $4,225. The SOXX semiconductor ETF showed a 6.6% spike, but a data-source conflict of 6.7% makes that move unreliable — use caution interpreting sector-level tech snapshots.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain hard-blocked on overbought technicals; US10Y at 4.45% still well above the 4.20% threshold for NIM relief. Within the cluster, OCBC is the relative outperformer on margin resilience but is equally extended, while UOB's discounted valuation is the most attractive if NIM compression eases. The Goldman Sachs food-supply shock warning for Southeast Asia (per CNBC, June 22) adds a fresh tail risk for net-importing Singapore.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally pressured — awaiting a sustained US10Y break below 4.20% or MAS easing.
singapore.key_development: US10Y slipped 0.27% to 4.45% but remains above the 4.20% NIM-relief threshold; Goldman warns of food-price shocks for net food importers Singapore and Philippines.

semiconductors.summary: Semis rallied hard into the weekend but strength remains concentrated in memory (SK Hynix +5.6%, MU premarket +3.2%) while logic/foundry names lag on China export-control retaliation against U.S. defense firms. The SOXX benchmark shows a 6.6% daily spike, but that figure is unreliable due to a 6.7% data-source conflict — trade the divergence with caution. Within the cluster, memory is the outperforming subsector, while ASML and AMD are extremely extended absent fresh catalysts.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish for memory, mixed for logic — AI capex demand is visible, but geopolitical frictions are broadening.
semiconductors.key_development: SK Hynix overtakes Samsung as Korea's most valuable company (per Reuters, June 22); China imposes export controls on 10 U.S. defense firms (per CNBC, June 22).

big_tech.summary: BigTech signals diverge sharply: GOOG upgraded to BUY on TPU strength and reasonable valuation, while MSFT remains CAUTION on the OpenAI revenue circularity and AMZN is HOLD pending a trend change. The hyperscaler capex debate is alive — Microsoft's Nadella publicly critiqued AI power concentration (per WSJ, June 22) while Amazon and Google both have massive AI infrastructure commitments that have yet to translate to earnings.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — cloud compute demand is real; the question is who monetises it cleanly.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT CEO Satya Nadella's WSJ interview critiques AI power balance; GOOG's Triggerfish chip with MediaTek (per Stocktwits, June 22) signals a custom-silicon acceleration.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA's $2B photonics investment (per NVIDIA IR, March 2026) — Coherent is the other half of the optical interconnect supply chain alongside LITE.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's next earnings confirm photonics revenue above $150M quarterly run-rate.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Samsung lost Korea's most-valuable-company crown to SK Hynix (per Reuters, June 22); HBM yield issues keep it behind in memory, but foundry wins could re-rate it.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after SK Hynix's next earnings for HBM demand signals and any Samsung HBM qualification news.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and datacenter networking play — directly tied to hyperscaler AI capex (AVGO, NVDA read-through).
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AVGO breaks above $412.95, confirming custom ASIC demand strength.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz re-closed by Iran citing Lebanon ceasefire violations (per World Oil, June 20); shipping traffic disrupted again, oil volatility elevated.
China imposes export controls on 10 U.S. defense firms and procurement bans on 46 others, retaliating for the Pentagon's 1260H blacklist expansion (per CNBC, June 22).
Fed Chair Warsh's hawkish hold signals a potential rate hike; market pricing 75% chance of a hike as early as September (per Yahoo Finance).
Goldman Sachs warns of a food-supply shock for Southeast Asia due to elevated oil and fertilizer costs and a potential El Niño (per CNBC, June 22).
geopolitical.market_impact: The renewed Hormuz closure injects immediate oil-price uncertainty, capping the peace-rally momentum in tech and keeping energy costs elevated. China's export-control retaliation broadens the U.S.-China tech war beyond semiconductors into defense supply chains, weighing on semis and BigTech sentiment. The hawkish Fed posture combined with Thursday's PCE inflation data keeps rate-sensitive names like Singapore banks and MSFT under pressure.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran re-closes Strait of Hormuz (per World Oil, June 20); China imposes fresh export restrictions on 10 U.S. defense firms (per CNBC, June 22).

base.description: Hormuz sees intermittent closures through the Swiss negotiations, keeping WTI choppy around $75-85. Fed holds through Q3 but PCE data on Thursday runs hot, reinforcing a September hike. Semis remain capped by extension and geopolitical friction; BigTech diverges with GOOG and NVDA outperforming on AI-specific catalysts.

optimistic.description: A durable Hormuz ceasefire in the coming weeks allows oil to stabilise below $70, and Thursday's PCE comes in below expectations, pushing rate-hike odds down. Semis and BigTech rally broadly, lifting overbought banks and allowing entry on pullbacks.

pessimistic.description: Hormuz closure persists, WTI spikes back above $90, and PCE surprises to the upside. The Fed hikes in July, crushing rate-sensitive sectors. Singapore banks sell off as NIM pressure intensifies; semis correct sharply on energy-cost and China-tariff fears.

wildcard.description: A significant cyberattack or military escalation in the Taiwan Strait, or a sudden U.S.-China deal on semiconductors that lifts all restrictions — either extreme would reprice the entire tech landscape overnight.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy capital cautiously into the two BUY signals (NVDA, GOOG) with half positions given tight stops; keep a cash reserve for the binary events this week (MU, CBRS, PCE).

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Selectively bullish in AI, defensive elsewhere
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 16 of 28 names are hard-blocked on technical extension. The renewed Hormuz closure and China export controls add fresh geopolitical risk, offsetting last week's peace-rally momentum. Two BUY signals (NVDA, GOOG) offer limited entry but with tight stops; the rest of the portfolio waits on pullbacks or binary-event resolutions.

macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Core PCE rises to 3.5%+, cementing a September hike; yields spike, semis sell off.

taxonomy_discrimination.note: Output-only meta-analysis of signal-class discrimination. NOT injected into the analysis prompt and NOT persisted into memory.lessons_learned. full_corpus pools pre-cutover (different-instrument) rows — informational only.

calibration_insights.confidence_banner: Actionable buckets BUY (n=1) / ACCUMULATE (n=8) spanning trend_up. NOT yet decision-grade — alpha figures are benchmark-relative; single-regime samples carry no out-of-regime evidence.
calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
